calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-11-2026 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Entire Action
Off Calendar Feb-10-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom), prior to Open Court:
Carolina Faccin, Esq. 619-599-8292 JCL Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Bo Kyung Kim, Esq. 415-433-1940 Littler Mendelson for Defendant.

Prior to the hearing Counsel sent a notice to contest the Court's TR clarifying the date of filing a proposed order. Counsel submitted a proposed order prior to Open Court. At the Court's instruction, the clerk informs parties the "intention" of the proposed order is to satisfy ambiguity. Parties state they have no further issues.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Keisha Garrett's unopposed Motion to Dismiss Entire Action is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing and a proper showing having been made under California Rules of Court, rule 3.770, the court orders Plaintiff may dismiss her action, including her class claims, without prejudice.

Moving Parties to execute and file CV 110 form(s) to effectuate dismissal. Moving Parties are advised to present this order to the clerk at the time of presenting the executed CV110 form(s).

Moving Parties must submit a proposed order that includes the above text verbatim no later than 8:30 am on 2/10/26, by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
